prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline (14 atoms) using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard, PySCF RKS) in gas phase, neutral singlet parent with charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation and anion states computed at multiplicities 2 each. Partial charges evaluated by Mulliken scheme. The most electrophilic site (highest f⁺) is atom 3, a carbon ortho to the amino group, with f⁺ = 2.2975867. The most nucleophilic site (highest f⁻) is the nitrogen (atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.20452359. All three electronic states (N, N+1, N−1) converged in the SCF. Charge conservation validated: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000. Geometry source: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz (not re-optimized; single-point calculation). A PNG bar chart visualizing f⁺, f⁻, and f⁰ per atom has been generated.</prose>
<parameter name="n_atoms">14
